name: NASA-Prompt-WorkPlan
description: NASA Work Plan section - task breakdown, milestones, timeline, team roles, and management approach

variants.name: default
variants.text:
You are writing the WORK PLAN section of a NASA grant proposal submitted in response to a NASA Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO). INPUTS: - {{content}}: The researcher's actual work, publications, and technical expertise. This is your PRIMARY source for understanding the research tasks, methods, and team capabilities. - {{grant_info}}: The specific NASA NOFO description, including period of performance, deliverables, and any management requirements. YOUR TASK: Generate ONLY the Work Plan section. This section covers the detailed task breakdown, timeline, milestones, team roles, and management approach. Do NOT generate the Scientific/Technical/Management Plan narrative or other proposal components. NOTE: These prompts are a best-effort starting point. The researcher should review and update all content — especially timelines, milestones, and personnel assignments — to reflect their actual planned work. SECTION STRUCTURE: 1. WORK BREAKDOWN STRUCTURE - Organize the proposed work into 3-6 major tasks or work packages - For each task: * Task name and number (e.g., Task 1: ...) * Objective: what this task will accomplish * Approach: how it will be executed, grounded in methods from {{content}} * Deliverables: specific outputs (data, models, publications, reports) * Dependencies: which other tasks must precede or run in parallel * Lead personnel: who is responsible - Tasks should map directly to the scientific objectives described in the Scientific/Technical/Management Plan - Ground all tasks in the researcher's actual capabilities from {{content}} 2. TIMELINE AND MILESTONES - Present a timeline covering the full period of performance from {{grant_info}} - For each task, identify: * Start month and end month (relative to award start, e.g., Month 1-6) * Key milestones with specific, measurable criteria for completion * Go/no-go decision points where applicable - Identify the critical path — which tasks are sequential vs. parallel - Include a Gantt chart description or table showing task overlap and sequencing - Milestones should be ambitious but realistic given the team and resources described in {{content}} 3. TEAM ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES - List all key personnel and their roles: * Principal Investigator (PI): overall scientific leadership and NASA reporting * Co-Investigators: specific technical responsibilities * Graduate students/postdocs: task assignments and training objectives * Collaborators: specific contributions and how they integrate with the core team - For each person, connect their expertise from {{content}} to their assigned tasks - Describe how the team will coordinate (meetings, reporting cadence, data sharing) 4. MANAGEMENT APPROACH - Describe the overall management structure - Explain how progress will be tracked against milestones - Describe the reporting plan (interim reports, final report, publications) - Identify any risks to schedule or scope and how they will be managed - If the proposal involves subcontractors or subawardees, describe the oversight approach - Describe how the team will respond if a task falls behind schedule 5. DELIVERABLES AND PRODUCTS - List all expected deliverables: * Scientific publications (peer-reviewed journals) * Conference presentations * Datasets (reference the Data Management Plan) * Software or tools (if applicable) * NASA technical reports or interim reports * Any hardware or prototype deliverables - For each deliverable, identify the target date and responsible personnel WRITING GUIDELINES: - Be specific and realistic — NASA reviewers evaluate whether the work plan is achievable - Every task should be traceable to the scientific objectives in the proposal - Do NOT fabricate personnel, facilities, or capabilities not described in {{content}} - If {{content}} does not provide sufficient detail about team composition or facilities, note what information the researcher should add - Define all acronyms on first use - Timelines should be relative to award start date (Month 1, Month 6, etc.) not calendar dates NASA EVALUATION CRITERIA THIS SECTION ADDRESSES: - Reasonableness and feasibility of the proposed work plan - Qualifications of the proposing team relative to the proposed tasks - Adequacy of the management approach - Reasonableness of the proposed budget relative to the proposed work OUTPUT FORMAT: Generate well-structured HTML: - Use

for the section title "Work Plan" - Use

for each major subsection - Use

for individual tasks - Use

for paragraph content - Use , , , ,
, for timeline and milestone tables - Use

    1. for lists - Use for task names and milestone labels - Target approximately 3-5 pages (2,000-3,500 words)
